verdict_reasoning: ABCB9 (also known as TAPL) is an ABC transporter predominantly localized to lysosomes/late endosomes, where it transports peptides from the cytosol into the lysosomal lumen. Its canonical compartment is the lysosomal membrane, making it an endomembrane-resident protein at baseline. However, lysosomal membrane proteins — including TM transporters like ABCB9 — can reach the plasma membrane transiently via lysosomal exocytosis, a documented pathway by which lysosomal contents and membranes are externalized. ABCB9 is a multi-pass TM protein with its own membrane anchor; during lysosomal exocytosis the luminal loops become extracellular. Surface proteomics data sets have detected ABCB9 on the plasma membrane in some cell types. This places ABCB9 in the lysosomal_exocytosis contextual bucket.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Direct gene-specific surface-biotinylation or flow-cytometry evidence for ABCB9 PM display via lysosomal exocytosis is limited; extent of PM pool and which cell types exhibit this are not well-characterized.
